narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text attacks the climate movement by using pejorative language, such as 'climate cultists', and questioning the validity of their claims, implying that they are using scare tactics to manipulate the public.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by suggesting that predictions of catastrophic consequences have not come true, implying that the issue is not as serious as claimed.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the alleged failure of predictions, implying that the scientific consensus is flawed or unreliable.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent climate advocate, for his alleged hypocrisy in using fossil fuels while advocating for their reduction, implying that climate leaders are not practicing what they preach.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text attacks the climate movement by using pejorative language, such as 'climate cultists', and mocking their terminology, implying that they are using scare tactics to manipulate the public.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by citing past failed predictions and implying that current warnings are also exaggerated or untrue.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent climate advocate, for his perceived hypocrisy and excessive use of fossil fuels, undermining his credibility and the credibility of climate institutions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: “Renewable energy ... is now the cheapest source of electricity in almost the entire planet,” Gore lied.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of Gore's statement about renewable energy, implying that he is misrepresenting or manipulating scientific data to support his claims.
